issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
subject: Subscription pause
response: Hello, I understand you'd like to pause your subscription as you've stopped hiring efforts. According to our Pause Subscription guide, this feature is available for individual self-serve plan subscribers. To use it, your subscription must have been active for at least 30 days and be a monthly subscription for either the Individual Monthly - Basic or Interview Monthly plan. If you meet these criteria, you can pause your subscription by accessing Subscription Settings, clicking Cancel Plan, and selecting a pause duration from 1 to 12 months. However, based on our available documentation, this feature does not apply to team or enterprise subscriptions. If your subscription is not an individual self-serve plan, we may need to explore other options, but our current documentation does not provide further details on pausing for other subscription types.
justification: The response is based solely on the Pause Subscription documentation, which outlines eligibility and steps for individual self-serve plans, and notes limitations for other subscription types.

issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
subject: Certificate name update
response: Hello! I understand you'd like to update the name on your certificate after completing an assessment. According to our Certifications FAQs, you can update the name on your certificate, but please note that this can only be done once per account, and the change will apply to all your certificates. To update it, please follow these steps: 1. Open your certificate page. 2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display. 3. Click Regenerate Certificate. 4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name. After this, your certificate will be updated with the new name. If you have any further issues, feel free to ask.
justification: Based on the Certifications FAQs document, which provides instructions for updating certificate names and notes the one-time change limitation.

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
Hello! I understand you'd like to stop Claude from crawling your website. According to our guide 'Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?', you can block our crawler by modifying your robots.txt file. Specifically, add the following to your top-level robots.txt file for each subdomain you wish to opt out from:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

This will prevent Anthropic's ClaudeBot from crawling your site. Please note that other methods like blocking IP addresses may not work reliably. If you have further questions or believe our bots are malfunctioning, you can reach out to claudebot@anthropic.com from an email that includes the domain you're contacting us about.
justification: This response is based on Document 2, which details how site owners can block Anthropic's crawler using robots.txt directives, including the specific example provided.
